remember: Recall the laws of refraction and reflection of light
List the different types of optical instruments and their applications
Identify the principles of formation of images in various optical instruments
understand: Explain the concept of wavefront and its significance in wave optics
Describe the formation of interference patterns in Young's double-slit experiment
Discuss the principle and working of a diffraction grating
apply: Use the laws of refraction and reflection to solve problems related to image formation
Apply the principles of interference and diffraction to solve problems related to wavefronts and optical instruments
Design and explain the working of a simple optical instrument using principles of wave optics
analyze: Analyze the factors affecting the formation of images in optical instruments
Compare and contrast the principles of formation of images in convex and concave lenses
Explain the limitations and applications of wave optics in various fields

ncert_objectives: Explain the formation of interference patterns in Young's double-slit experiment.
Describe the principle and working of a diffraction grating.
Use the laws of refraction and reflection to derive the equation of the focal length of a lens.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the formation of interference patterns in Young's double-slit experiment.
Derive the equation of the focal length of a lens using the laws of refraction and reflection.
Describe the working of a diffraction grating and its applications.
